missions.title: Morphology of Flowering Plants
missions.description: Comprehensive study of root, stem, leaf modifications, inflorescence, flower structure, and families.

microtargets.title: Root and Stem Modifications
ncertAnchors: NCERT Biology Class 11, Chapter: Morphology of Flowering Plants
mustKnowFacts: Prop roots (Banyan) support the tree; stilt roots (Maize, Sugarcane) arise from lower nodes.
Pneumatophores (Rhizophora) grow upward for oxygen.
Stem tendrils (Gourds, Grapevine) develop from axillary buds.
Thorns (Citrus, Bougainvillea) are modified axillary buds.

diagrams.name: Modifications of root
commonLabelTraps: Confusing adventitious roots with tap roots

commonTraps: Confusing potato (stem modification) with sweet potato (root modification).
Thinking thorns are leaves (they are stem modifications, spines are leaf modifications).
masteryCriteria: Identify the plant part modified for storage, support or protection.
Differentiate between stem and root modifications.
pyqPatterns: Matching plants with their specific morphological modifications.
Identifying homologous vs analogous structures based on morphology.

activeRecallQuestions.question: What is the origin of stem tendrils in grapevines?

microtargets.title: Leaf Venation and Phyllotaxy
ncertAnchors: NCERT Biology Class 11, Chapter: Morphology of Flowering Plants
mustKnowFacts: Reticulate venation is typical of dicots; parallel venation is typical of monocots.
Phyllotaxy types: Alternate (China rose), Opposite (Calotropis, Guava), Whorled (Alstonia).
commonTraps: Confusing alternate and opposite phyllotaxy.
masteryCriteria: Recognize phyllotaxy from descriptions or images.
pyqPatterns: Examples of phyllotaxy are frequently asked.

activeRecallQuestions.question: Which plant exhibits whorled phyllotaxy?

missions.title: Mastery Module 1: Root morphology and modifications and Related Concepts
missions.description: Comprehensive analysis covering root morphology and modifications, stem morphology and modifications, leaf morphology and venation and advanced applications.

microtargets.title: Root morphology and modifications
ncertAnchors: NCERT Biology Class 11, Chapter: Structural Organisation in Animals and Plants, Section detailing Root morphology and modifications
mustKnowFacts: A thorough understanding of Root morphology and modifications is frequently appears in NEET examinations.
Applying the core principles of Root morphology and modifications correctly is essential for solving integrated numerical and conceptual problems in Structural Organisation in Animals and Plants.

diagrams.name: Anatomy of root morphology and modifications

commonTraps: Misinterpreting the advanced application scenarios of Root morphology and modifications during high-pressure exam scenarios.
Failing to recall the exact conditions or sign conventions associated with Root morphology and modifications.
masteryCriteria: Can accurately solve numericals or identify biological structures related specifically to Root morphology and modifications.
Successfully avoids common pitfalls when Root morphology and modifications is integrated with other concepts in Structural Organisation in Animals and Plants.
pyqPatterns: NEET PyQs emphasize the application of Root morphology and modifications in tricky assertion-reason or statement-based questions.

activeRecallQuestions.question: Explain an application where using the concept of Root morphology and modifications can lead to a common student error.
